notes: Cumulative knowledge base for monthly sustainability news. Add new entries each month and reference related items.

policy.title: Energy Omnibus XII published (24 June 2026)
policy.summary: Commission publishes Omnibus XII simplifying energy labelling and digital EPREL product registry rules — implementation runway for retailers and equipment buyers.
impact: Clearer efficiency labels when comparing kitchen and building equipment
Digital registry work affects how product data is published before Q3 compliance
differences: Legislative text published — moves from consultation to implementation planning

policy.title: CBAM: two months to the annual declaration
policy.summary: Importers approaching the August 2026 CBAM annual declaration deadline — Q3 is the critical window to validate embedded emissions data.
impact: Higher carbon imports cost more at the border
Efficient domestic equipment becomes more competitive vs high-carbon imports
differences: Focus on annual declaration runway rather than regime start

policy.title: Circular Economy Act: Q3 proposal runway
policy.summary: Commission preparing Q3 2026 Circular Economy Act proposal after May–June stakeholder workshops on secondary raw materials and critical materials recovery.
impact: More predictable circular procurement standards for equipment and materials
Planning window for repair, reuse, and verified circular sourcing
differences: Post-College political phase with Q3 legislative target

policy.title: CBAM Definitive Regime Begins
policy.summary: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism moves to definitive phase with broader product coverage.
differences: Moves from reporting phase to certificate obligations

policy.title: CSRD Wave 1 Reporting Complete
policy.summary: Large companies completed first CSRD reporting cycle under ESRS standards.
impact: More supplier data requests from large clients
Energy and carbon reporting expectations increase
differences: Wave 1 completed; next waves preparing

policy.title: Energy Omnibus Package (Q2 2026)
policy.summary: Simplified energy product rules expected to reduce compliance burden.
differences: Consolidation of rules into a single package

policy.title: Circular Economy Act Timeline Confirmed
policy.summary: Timeline confirmed for the Circular Economy Act in 2026.
differences: Timeline focus rather than new policy detail

policy.title: Circular Economy Act (Q3 2026)
policy.summary: Expected adoption in Q3 2026 to establish a single market for circular products.
differences: Quarter-specific timing confirmed

policy.title: Circular Economy Act (2026)
policy.summary: Commission plans a 2026 Act to strengthen the single market for circular products, waste, and secondary raw materials.
impact: More consistent circular product requirements across EU markets
Lower friction for verified circular sourcing
differences: Moves from pilot measures to a single‑market framework

policy.title: Circular economy measures package (Dec 2025)
policy.summary: First package of circular measures with pilots on plastics, circularity hubs, and competitiveness coordination.
impact: Pilot actions create early pathways for circular procurement
Signals which areas will scale in 2026
differences: Pilot focus rather than binding single‑market rules

policy.title: EEA 8th EAP indicator assessment (2025)
policy.summary: EEA reports many circularity indicators remain off‑track despite emissions progress.
impact: Greater pressure for circularity reporting and action
More scrutiny on waste, material use, and product lifecycles
differences: Indicator‑based assessment rather than new legislation

funding.title: Clean Industrial Deal Launches (€540M)
funding.summary: Horizon Europe allocates €540M for Clean Industrial Deal calls in 2026-2027.
impact: More retrofit and efficiency funding options
differences: Dedicated call within Horizon Europe budget

funding.title: LIFE Programme Awards (€358M)
funding.summary: LIFE Programme results include significant circular economy funding.
differences: Award results vs open calls

funding.title: Horizon Europe 2026–2027
impact: More calls for innovation and retrofit projects
differences: Two-year budget cycle emphasis

funding.title: LIFE Programme 2025
funding.summary: LIFE continues to fund circular economy and quality of life projects.
differences: Ongoing program vs award results

funding.title: Cohesion Policy — Circular Economy
funding.summary: Cohesion Policy supports circular economy investments via ERDF and Cohesion Fund.
differences: Regional implementation focus

funding.title: EIB Climate Bank Roadmap Phase 2 (2026‑2030)
funding.summary: EIB sets a €30B adaptation finance target and confirms 50%+ climate/environment financing share.
impact: More funding for resilience and retrofit projects
Projects framed as adaptation may score higher
differences: Higher adaptation focus and simplification emphasis

funding.title: IDAE €202.5M renewables & storage call (Spain)
funding.summary: Second call for innovative renewables and storage opens Jan 14 and closes Feb 19, 2026.
impact: Grants can reduce capex for heat pumps and storage
Preference for EU‑made equipment supports local supply
differences: Focused on innovative projects and specific lines like agrivoltaics

circular.title: Netherlands Leads Electronics Circularity
circular.summary: Digital product passports, AI sorting, and refurbishment programs expand circular electronics.
differences: Specific spotlight on electronics initiatives

circular.title: Circular Bio-based Europe (CBE JU)
circular.summary: Public-private partnership funding bio-based circular innovation.
differences: Bio-based focus

circular.title: Circular Buildings & Construction
circular.summary: EU missions support circular construction and retrofit workflows.
impact: Lower construction waste and reuse gains
differences: Construction sector focus

circular.title: Sustronics Electronics Project
circular.summary: EU project redesigning electronics for reuse and recyclability.
differences: Electronics eco-design focus

circular.title: Netherlands electronics circularity initiatives
circular.summary: Dutch initiatives highlight digital product passports, AI‑enabled sorting, and refurbishment programs.
differences: Country‑specific implementation focus

countries.title: Netherlands ISDE updates (2026)
countries.summary: ISDE rules updated for ventilation, heat pumps, and biobased insulation; applications open 5 Jan 2026.
impact: More incentives for ventilation when paired with insulation
differences: New eligibility rules and updated thresholds

countries.title: UK Warm Home Discount extension
countries.summary: £150 annual rebate extended for the rest of the decade.
impact: Lower energy bills for eligible households
differences: Extension length and continued eligibility emphasis
